title: Deep frontend integration: 10+ backend analytics wired into stock detail, portfolio, and screener pages

observation.gap_identified: Backend enriches stock analysis with 10+ analytics sections (insider signal, liquidity score, momentum rank, technical analysis, drawdown analysis, factor exposure, trends attention, crash probability, relative valuation, conformal intervals) but frontend stock detail page displayed NONE of them. Portfolio page had risk_number and factor_exposures in API response but didn't display them. Screener was missing crash probability and confidence columns.

implementation.feature_built: Wired 10+ standalone backend analytics into 3 user-facing frontend pages: (1) Stock detail page now shows Technical Analysis card (RSI, trend direction, TA signal components), Crash Risk Assessment with conformal intervals, Intelligence Dashboard row (liquidity score+tier, momentum rank percentile, insider signal+cluster buy detection, Google Trends attention), Drawdown Analysis (max DD, avg recovery, rolling Sharpe/Sortino), Factor Exposure (FF5 alpha, R-squared, style tags), and Relative Valuation vs peers. (2) Portfolio page now displays Risk Number (1-100 Bloomberg PORT-style gauge with category badge) and Factor Exposures (portfolio-level FF5 decomposition with per-holding betas). (3) Screener table now includes Crash 3M probability and Signal Confidence columns with color-coded thresholds.

limitations: Frontend-only changes — no new backend endpoints or services created
TypeScript types for enriched fields use optional chaining (graceful degradation if backend doesn't return them)
No new test coverage for frontend components (React testing not set up)
Relative valuation metrics display assumes specific backend response shape
assessment.self_critique: Could have also wired portfolio attribution (Brinson-Fachler) and MCTR into the portfolio page as additional tabs/sections. The stock detail page is getting long — could benefit from a tabbed layout in a future cycle. Should verify the actual backend response shapes match the TypeScript types more carefully.

next_steps: Add portfolio attribution (Brinson-Fachler) and MCTR display to portfolio page
Add tabbed layout to stock detail page to organize the growing number of sections
Wire sector rotation data from market-status into the dashboard page
Add stress testing results to stock detail or portfolio pages
Consider adding interactive charts for drawdown and factor exposure data
